mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and dampens corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when FEDFUNDS is above 5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power diminishes significantly during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with stable, low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a healthy consumer demand environment without overheating, fostering strong corporate revenues and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change < 3%
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when RSAFS is growing and CPIAUCSL is low, or the interaction term between these two proxies is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, translating into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth does not correlate with positive SPX returns during identified expansionary regimes, or the 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer and business confidence in the real estate sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns following significant HOUST declines in more than 40% of occurrences, or the signal's predictive power is not robust across different economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative or flat during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and SPX returns turns negative for any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a strong monetary tightening response to inflationary pressures, which typically increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate profits and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns following periods of strong GDPC1 growth in more than 35% of occurrences, or the signal's predictive power is not consistent across different expansionary phases.
